lace_function\",\"source\":\"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):\\n    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:\\n        return None\\n    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100\\n    return optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=max(1, total_steps))\\n\",\"text\":\"\"}],\"primary_mode\":\"micro\",\"rationale\":\"With a fixed 256-step budget, the baseline only processes 16,384 examples at batch size 64. Raising batch size to 256 lets the model see about 65,536 examples within the same step budget, which is usually the highest-leverage safe change on CIFAR-10 here. A higher Adam learning rate and a cosine schedule over the actual step budget compensate for the larger batch and improve optimization within the short horizon.\",\"secondary_modes\":[\"topk\",\"summaries\"]}"}}
